data.title: Cirtek Moves to Replace PwC Amid Audit Dispute and Results Delay
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest announcement is out from Cirtek Holdings Limited ( (HK:1433) ).

Cirtek Holdings plans to remove PricewaterhouseCoopers as its auditor and appoint Grant Thornton Hong Kong, subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting, after a dispute over the handling of an investigation into certain payments by an overseas subsidiary. The disagreement, centered on audit arrangements and the composition of the Bangladesh component audit team, has contributed to a delay in publishing the 2025 annual results and a continued suspension of trading in the company’s shares, underscoring governance and transparency concerns for investors and regulators.

PwC had raised red flags over payments recorded under property, plant and equipment and other expenses, recommending an independent investigation by reputable professional investigators and legal counsel. The firm stated it had not received sufficient information about the proposed investigators, legal advisers, or the scope of the probe to assess their competence and independence, and it maintained that the existing component audit team, which identified the issues, should remain in place, highlighting a serious breakdown in alignment between the auditor and the company’s management and audit committee.

More about Cirtek Holdings Limited

Cirtek Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong, with operations that include overseas subsidiaries such as in Bangladesh. The group’s activities involve owning and managing property, plant and equipment and incurring related operating expenses across its international footprint, exposing it to multi-jurisdictional audit and compliance requirements.

Average Trading Volume: 1,424,285

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$162M

Learn more about 1433 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Dekon Controlling Shareholder Raises H-Share Stake in Confidence Move
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Dekon Food And Agriculture Group Class H ( (HK:2419) ) just unveiled an update.

Dekon Food and Agriculture Group announced that its controlling shareholder, chairman and executive director, Wang Degen, has increased his stake in the company by purchasing 648,300 H shares on the open market between 27 and 29 May 2026. The transaction, executed at an average price of about HK$52.07 per share for a total consideration of roughly HK$33.76 million, raised his interest in the company’s issued share capital by approximately 0.1667%.

The move is presented by Wang as a show of confidence in the group’s long-term development and growth prospects, signaling management’s positive view of the company’s future performance and potentially supporting market sentiment toward the stock. The board also reminded shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in the company’s shares, underscoring the inherent risks associated with equity investment despite the insider share purchase.

More about Dekon Food And Agriculture Group Class H

Dekon Food and Agriculture Group is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China that operates in the food and agriculture sector. Listed in Hong Kong as an H-share issuer, the group focuses on integrated agricultural and livestock-related food businesses, serving both domestic and international markets through its subsidiaries.

data.title: CTR Holdings Tightens Nomination Committee Rules to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

CTR Holdings Limited ( (HK:1416) ) has issued an update.

CTR Holdings Limited has updated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, originally established in 2019, to ensure continued compliance with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s Listing Rules and to formalize its governance framework around board appointments. The revised terms specify that the committee must comprise at least three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, include at least one member of a different gender, and clarify procedures for appointments, removals, meeting notices, and tenure, underscoring the company’s focus on board diversity, transparency, and regulatory alignment.

The updated mandate also sets out clear rules for convening meetings, including notice periods and acceptable communication methods, which is expected to streamline committee operations and enhance accountability in nomination processes. These changes reinforce CTR Holdings’ corporate governance structure, potentially improving investor confidence by demonstrating a structured approach to board oversight and diversity as regulatory expectations in Hong Kong continue to evolve.

More about CTR Holdings Limited

CTR Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operating under the governance framework required for Hong Kong-listed issuers. Its board structure includes committees such as the Nomination Committee, which oversees director appointments and board composition to align with regulatory standards and corporate governance best practices.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

data.title: Ever Reach to Sell 70% Stake in Property Subsidiary for RMB3 Million
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:3616) ) has provided an update.

Ever Reach Group has agreed to sell its entire 70% equity stake in Xuchang Yuanda Real Estate Company Limited, an indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, to Xuchang Fengzhida Real Estate Company Limited for RMB3 million. The disposal means Ever Reach will cease to hold any interest in the target, which will no longer be consolidated into the group’s financial statements, slightly reshaping its asset base and project exposure.

The transaction is classified as both a discloseable and connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules because the purchaser is a substantial shareholder of the target and therefore a connected person at the subsidiary level. With board approval and confirmation by independent non-executive directors that the terms are fair, reasonable and on normal commercial terms, the deal requires only public announcement, streamlining execution while signaling governance safeguards for minority shareholders.

More about Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd.

Ever Reach Group (Holdings) Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through subsidiaries in the mainland China property sector. Its business involves real estate development and related property interests, with holdings in various project companies that it consolidates into its group financial statements.

Shareholders will vote on reappointing KPMG as the company’s auditor, with an estimated 2026 audit fee of RMB3.5 million to RMB4.5 million, a figure the company describes as arm’s length and reflective of the group’s size, complexity and audit scope. 51 Credit Card Inc. ( (HK:2051) ) has shared an update.

Vala Inc. has convened its annual general meeting for 24 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and approve the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. The meeting will also consider the re-election of executive director Sun Haitao and independent non-executive director Xu Xuchu, authorize the board to set directors’ remuneration, re-appoint Zhonghui Anda CPA Limited as auditor, and approve a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, excluding certain standard exceptions.

These resolutions, if passed, will refresh the company’s authorization to raise equity capital and manage its share base flexibly, including issuing new shares under a capped general mandate in line with Hong Kong listing rules. The governance items, such as director re-elections and auditor re-appointment, underscore continuity in oversight while preserving the board’s ability to respond to future financing or strategic needs, with implications for potential share dilution and capital structure management closely watched by investors.

More about 51 Credit Card Inc.

Vala Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2051. It operates under the Hong Kong listing regime and is governed by a board of directors that includes executive and independent non-executive members, reflecting standard corporate governance practices for Hong Kong–listed issuers.

Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd. ( (HK:1273) ) has provided an update.

Hong Kong Finance Group has agreed to extend a new secured loan of HK$4 million to three repeat customers under a nine-month facility carrying an interest rate of 18% per annum. The loan is backed by a second mortgage over two residential units and two car parking spaces on Conduit Road, Hong Kong, valued at about HK$148 million by an independent valuer.

Including this advance, the group has provided a total of HK$60 million in loans to these borrowers within a year, requiring aggregation under Hong Kong listing rules and triggering a discloseable transaction classification. The company says the short tenure, strong collateral coverage, and solid financial background and repayment history of key borrowers render the credit risk manageable for its lending portfolio.

More about Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd.

Hong Kong Finance Group Limited is a Hong Kong-based financial services company engaged in money lending activities through its subsidiary HK Finance. The group focuses on providing secured loans to customers, often backed by real estate collateral in prime locations, and operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: RUSAL Pays Fifteenth Coupon on 30 Million Ruble Bonds
data.description:

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has provided an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that it paid coupon income on its 001PC-05 series non-documentary commercial bonds maturing in September 2027, issued under its 001РС commercial bonds programme. The bonds, with a nominal value of 1,000 rubles each and a total of 30 million outstanding, carry ISIN RU000A10B065.

For the fifteenth coupon period running from April 28 to May 28, 2026, the company paid a total of 374.4 million rubles in interest to bondholders. The coupon amounted to an annualized rate of 15.18%, equivalent to 12.48 rubles per bond, and was settled in non-cash Russian ruble payments on May 28, 2026, underscoring RUSAL’s ongoing servicing of its domestic debt obligations.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is a major international aluminium producer, operating as a public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation. The company is listed in Hong Kong, Moscow and St. Petersburg, providing investors with exposure to the global aluminium market and related financial instruments.

data.title: Redsun Services Flags Disputed Shareholder Meeting, Board Changes and Trading Suspension
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest update is out from Redsun Services Group Ltd ( (HK:1971) ).

Redsun Services Group Limited has disclosed that receivers and requisitioning shareholders have sent the company purported minutes and voting results from an adjourned extraordinary general meeting held on 28 May 2026, claiming that all proposed resolutions were passed. The resolutions include the removal of executive director Jia Hongbo and three independent non-executive directors, and the appointment of Tai Shaw Hoong as an executive director, all purportedly approved with 100% of votes cast in favour and none against.

The board highlights that there are serious questions over the validity of the entire extraordinary general meeting process, including its adjournment and the documents used to convene it, which may render the proceedings and the claimed resolutions invalid. In light of this corporate governance dispute and continuing uncertainty around control and board composition, trading in the company’s shares has been suspended, affecting shareholders and potential investors while the situation is clarified.

More about Redsun Services Group Ltd

Redsun Services Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through subsidiaries as part of the broader property and services sector. Its shares are traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1971, and its shareholder base includes parties that have recently enforced security interests over pledged shares.

data.title: Goldlion Wins Strong Shareholder Backing at 2026 AGM
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An update from Goldlion Holdings ( (HK:0533) ) is now available.

Goldlion Holdings reported that all resolutions put to shareholders at its 2026 annual general meeting on 29 May were approved by poll, including the adoption of the 2025 audited accounts and the declaration of a final dividend. Shareholders also backed the re-election of independent non-executive director Lo Wing Sze, reappointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and the presence of key board members was noted despite one absence due to illness.

Investors granted the board renewed mandates to repurchase shares, issue and allot new shares, and extend the issue mandate to cover repurchased stock, reinforcing the company’s capital management flexibility. With all ordinary resolutions passing by comfortable margins across a 973.8 million-share base, the AGM outcome underlines continued shareholder support for Goldlion’s governance structure and financial policies.

More about Goldlion Holdings

Goldlion Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group operates in the branded apparel and accessories sector, with a focus on Greater China and regional Asian markets, targeting mid- to upper-tier consumers through its own retail network and distribution partners.

data.title: Xinyuan Property Management Ousts Department President Over Unauthorized Communications
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. ( (HK:1895) ) has provided an announcement.

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. has removed Mr. Wang Yantao as president of its property management department, effective 29 May 2026, and the board said the change is not expected to adversely affect the group’s operations. The move follows the board’s finding that, during his tenure, Mr. Wang took part in external communication activities in which materials were released without proper internal authorization, prompting the company to clarify that any such unauthorized communications do not represent its views and to pledge further strengthening of governance and authorization systems to protect shareholder interests.

The company stressed that only formally authorized statements, meeting records, and communications reflect its official positions, distancing itself from any prior unauthorized disclosures linked to Mr. Wang’s activities. It reiterated its commitment to corporate governance and internal control and said it will continue reviewing and tightening internal governance and authorization mechanisms, signalling to investors and other stakeholders that it aims to contain potential reputational or compliance risks arising from unauthorized public communications.

More about Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd.

Xinyuan Property Management Service (Cayman) Ltd. is a Hong Kong–listed property management group incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The company focuses on providing property management and related services, operating through a group structure that serves property owners and communities, with an emphasis on corporate governance and internal control in its management practices.
